title: Judge sets murder suspect's conditions, bail at $500,000
summary: The parents of a young man beaten to death last week at an Eagle River home urged a judge during a hearing Thursday not to let one of the three men accused in the killing out of jail.

title: Export-Import Bank Staying Open Despite Expiring Charter
summary: By law, it must manage its existing portfolio of loans, loan guarantees and insurance to help foreign customers buy products from American exporters. But now it cannot make new deals.

title: Mount Marathon lottery: Elation, and agony
summary: Each year, hundreds of runners apply for the Mount Marathon lottery. The lucky ones rejoice, the unlucky ones endure.

title: 'SuperTrash' exhibit celebrates obscure art of B-movie posters
summary: "SuperTrash," opening today at the Anchorage Museum, is a collection of B-movie posters on loan from the Andy Warhol Museum in Pittsburgh. Some are campy and funny, while many others are risqué and, well, trashy.

title: Nordic-Baltic-Polish 9 should boost security, think tank says
summary: Nine countries -- the five Nordic nations, the three Baltic nations and Poland -- now make up a new front line to Russia’s “provocation and intimidation," the Center for European Policy Analysis says.

title: Environmental regulation could change Shell's plan for drilling in Alaska's Arctic
summary: A regulation meant to protect Arctic walruses prohibits simultaneous operation of two drill rigs within 15 miles of each other. That means Shell's Chukchi well sites, which are clustered together, likely won't be drilled simultaneously, an Interior spokeswoman says.

title: The donut hole at the center of the Arctic Ocean
summary: The 1.1 million-square-mile international zone at the top of the Arctic is a global commons, and there is emerging international support to protect it.

title: Alaska honors VPSO Thomas Madole, killed fulfilling a calling
summary: A Sunday memorial service for Thomas O. Madole, a Village Police Safety Officer who was killed in the line of duty last week, painted a more detailed portrait of a man who sacrificed his life in fulfillment of a calling.

title: More draft regulations for Alaska's marijuana industry unveiled
summary: At the Marijuana Control Board's first meeting Thursday, it laid out draft regulations covering marijuana retail operations and business licensing.

title: Feds return management of Kuskokwim to state amid low salmon numbers
summary: Federal managers had limited subsistence fishing and closed all fishing to nonrural residents -- meaning that visitors, newcomers and residents from outside 32 federally designated villages were not allowed to fish with any gear type. Under state management, that ban is lifted.

title: 'Star-studded' field to challenge for Mount Marathon women's title
summary: Pair of rookies, established stars expected to challenge for the crown.

title: Sand Point men sentenced for breaking into post office, disrupting deliveries
summary: Two young men from Sand Point in Southwest Alaska have been sentenced to nearly two years in prison for burglarizing the town’s post office late last year.

title: Houston prohibits fireworks as state pressed to restore broader bans
summary: Houston, one of the few places in Southcentral Alaska where fireworks are generally legal, has officially banned their use and sales over the Fourth of July weekend, even as the state announced it was lifting a broader suspension of fireworks sales and canceling a burn prohibition across most of the state.

title: Anchorage man managed prostitution business in several states, troopers say
summary: Mark Daniel Alexander, 31, of Anchorage, was arrested in Nevada and faces multiple sex trafficking charges after a months-long investigation by Alaska State Troopers.

title: Gas huffing suspected in Gambell man's death in Anchorage park
summary: Mike Albertson remembers his brother-in-law Kevin Lee Oozevaseuk as a talented Alaska Native carver, but one who suffered from a long history of huffing to get high.

title: Readers write: Letters to the editor, June 23, 2015
summary: LETTERS: Alaska Dispatch News readers take time out of a busy summer to weigh in on important matters.

title: Amanda Coyne: Parnell's light seems to be flickering
summary: Until recently, Gov. Sean Parnell has had a relatively soft ride, with the public and Alaska media taking it comparatively easy on our well-coiffed, unassuming governor. The one who declared during his 2008 race against U.S. Rep. Don Young that "I just want to be a light." A little odd, and he wasn't elected, but it sounded good. Who doesn't like light?

title: Rapidly moving Greenland glacier raises questions about long-term impacts
summary: The melting Sermeq Kujalleq glacier drains about 6 percent of the massive Greenland ice sheet and is already contributing to sea-level rise.

title: Anchorage burn ban still in effect
summary: The danger of setting a wildfire this summer has stayed high with hot, dry weather and wind, city fire officials said in a statement Thursday. Anyone caught burning is subject to a $75 fine.

title: Ling and Louie's blends Asian, American and fun
summary: Ling and Louie's Asian Bar and Grill bills its cuisine as "modern Asian meets American comfort." In other words, this chain restaurant is capitalizing on the Asian fusion trend that began on the West Coast and has since swept nation. With its sizable Filipino, Korean, Chinese and Laotian population, Anchorage seemed a natural fit for such a restaurant: While we love our moose burgers and Valley-grown spuds, we're equally at home dining onadobo, bulgogi and Peking duck.

title: Alaska legislator criticizes state corporation's charity golf tournament
summary: A state-owned corporation on Monday responded to a Wasilla legislator who criticized it for sponsoring a fundraising golf tournament during business hours as Alaska confronts a multibillion-dollar budget deficit.

title: Snowden ceremony shouldn’t take place in Barents Arctic, official says
summary: A proposal for an award ceremony to be held in September in the Norway-Russia border region is not being well-received by one local Norwegian official.

title: Alaska freezes inflation-linked payment increases to Medicaid providers
summary: Citing a “significantly underfunded” budget for the new fiscal year, the Alaska Department of Health and Social Services has told Medicaid providers they won’t be seeing the usual rate increases for inflation.

title: Alaska Dispatch and adn.com combining in expanded site
summary: Readers of Alaska Dispatch and adn.com will see big changes online this week. On Tuesday, we’re combining them into a single, expanded site that will be Alaska’s most complete and up-to-date source for news, features and commentary.

title: Missing family likely died in murder-suicide, Kenai police say
summary: Findings from the State Medical Examiner Office suggest a family of four, missing on the Kenai Peninsula for nearly a year before their remains were discovered in March, were the victims of a murder-suicide, Kenai police said Wednesday.

title: Berkowitz sworn in as Anchorage mayor in downtown ceremony
summary: Ethan Berkowitz was sworn in as Anchorage’s new mayor Wednesday afternoon in a downtown inauguration ceremony that incorporated his family and displayed the cultural diversity of the city.

title: Juneau airport ready to use resurfaced runway
summary: Planes will be able to land on the newly resurfaced runway at the Juneau airport for the first time Wednesday.

title: Walker names appointees for Alaska's first Marijuana Control Board
summary: Alaska's Marijuana Control Board will craft regulations surrounding legalized recreational and commercial marijuana. Of 132 applicants vying for the spots, here are the five chosen by Gov. Bill Walker.

title: Melting Arctic ice could be disrupting oceans' circulation - with major consequences
summary: As Arctic sea ice melts, it could disrupt a powerful Atlantic Ocean current that regulates temperatures in Western Europe and the East Coast of North America.

title: Early morning crash injures 1, disrupts power in Valley
summary: Alaska State Troopers said a car crashed into electrical wires early Wednesday morning, sending one person to the hospital. The crash disrupted power to 5,900 members, Matanuska Electric Association said.

title: Gov. Walker uses veto to delay payment of some oil tax credits
summary: Alaska Gov. Bill Walker signed state operating and capital budgets Monday, approving $9.8 billion in spending sought by the Legislature but vetoing a possible $200 million in controversial oil tax credits.

title: Anchorage fireworks display canceled as burn bans lifted for much of Alaska
summary: A burn ban was lifted Tuesday in many parts of Alaska but remains in place in Anchorage and Western Alaska, the Division of Forestry announced.

title: Pilot's body found amid wreckage of Southwest Alaska plane crash
summary: Blaze Highlander, 47, of Olympia, Washington, had taken a company Cessna 207 on a post-maintenance check flight from the company's Bethel base Saturday but never returned.

title: Anglo American withdraws from Pebble Mine, casting project's future into doubt
summary: Mining conglomerate Anglo American announced Monday it will abandon its 50 percent share in the Pebble Partnership, raising questions about the future of the controversial Pebble mine project.

title: Alaska offers $50 million, hoping to join feds in probing ANWR's oil prospects
summary: The 1002 area of ANWR off Alaska's North Slope may contain as much as 10 billion barrels of oil, and Gov. Sean Parnell is offering $50 million to join the feds in exploring it.

title: AACCC introduces new training program to create pit bull ambassadors
summary: Anchorage Animal Care and Control, StubbyDog, and Alaska Dog Sports are partnering to help pit bulls adopted from AACCC become ambassadors of the breed.

title: Winners and losers in Alaska's primary election
summary: Voters were in no mood to revive a coastal management program that lawmakers allowed to lapse. But the stage was set for a very interesting battle this fall for the Alaska Legislature.

title: Anchorage man managed prostitution business in several states, troopers say
summary: Mark Daniel Alexander, 31, of Anchorage, was arrested in Nevada and faces multiple sex trafficking charges after a months-long investigation by Alaska State Troopers.

title: 'Mind blowing' flames destroy homes in Washington state
summary: From just across the Wenatchee River, Dominick Bonny watched a whole neighborhood in his central Washington town burn as a wildfire destroyed two dozen structures and forced hundreds to flee.

title: What scares the toughest athletes in Alaska? Cow parsnip
summary: Not much frightens Brad Precosky. The Anchorage mountain runner has won the Mount Marathon race six times, negotiating perilous downhills with grace. But a tall green plant along trails scares the bejesus out of him.

title: Alaska Office of Children's Services under scrutiny in wake of Bethel sex-abuse allegations
summary: About one-third of social workers quit the Alaska Office of Children's Services western region each year, a turnover rate that's "severely detrimental" and potentially leaving children exposed to life-altering events or abuse, according to a review by the panel overseeing the state agency.

title: New rules will make home loans harder to get
summary: Financing a home is all about risk. If you have been waiting for house prices to drop more so you can take advantage of historic low interest rates, you risk losing out.

title: Alaska House primary election primer
summary: Redistricting has made some interesting races in the Alaska House, where all 40 lawmakers are up for election. Take a look at what's going on where you live.

title: Anchorage woman airlifted after raft flips on Placer River
summary: The 63-year-old woman sustained cold shock and a possible back injury after the raft she was in flipped on a Kenai Peninsula river Saturday, Alaska State Troopers said.

title: Alaska, other states sue EPA over controversial water rule
summary: Alaska and at least 15 other states are taking the Environmental Protection Agency to court over the agency's controversial "waters of the United States" regulation.

title: Alaska governor signs first tax increase in 10 years
summary: Governor Bill Walker signed a measure placing new taxes on wholesale refined fuel, including gasoline and heating oil -- but not aviation fuel or fuel used by the Alaska Marine Highway -- into law Saturday morning.

title: Greenland, Alaska share potential and problems of the Arctic Commons
summary: OPINION: Alaska and Greenland can learn from each other as partners in the north.

title: Alaska Energy Authority to explore LNG options for communities not on gas line
summary: The AEA is in the early phases of examining if liquefied natural gas might be a long-term solution for Alaska communities that won't have direct access to a proposed natural gas pipeline.

title: Recovery of Southeast Alaska plane crash wreckage could take weeks
summary: It could be weeks before investigators are able to move aircraft wreckage from the rugged site of a Southeast Alaska plane crash that killed nine people on a sightseeing tour out of Ketchikan Thursday.

title: We know it's not McKinley, but is Denali the right name for our mountain?
summary: OPINION: The rightful name of the tallest mountain in North America is Dghelay Ka’a. It's OK, there's a pronunciation guide.

title: Police: Anchorage shooting may be drug-related
summary: Anchorage police say they have not made any arrests in a Monday-night shooting in Mountain View in which the victim and assailant appeared to have known each other.

title: Environmental regulation could change Shell's plan for drilling in Alaska's Arctic
summary: A regulation meant to protect Arctic walruses prohibits simultaneous operation of two drill rigs within 15 miles of each other. That means Shell's Chukchi well sites, which are clustered together, likely won't be drilled simultaneously, an Interior spokeswoman says.

title: Lynne Curry: Pot's legal, but I got fired for using it anyway. Can I sue?
summary: We legalized pot in this state in February, so can you tell me how I got fired for THC on my drug test last week? I smoke in my house and on my own time and it’s none of my employer’s business. My buddies tell me I should sue my employer for violating my rights. Can I?

title: Bicyclist struck by car in Hillside accident
summary: Police said the bicyclist was eastbound on O'Malley Road in an area where there are no bicycle lanes and was struck while trying to make a left turn.

title: Anchorage woman gets 3 years for ties to cache of stolen weapons
summary: Megan Lindsey O’Connor, 26, was sentenced in federal court to three years for having a silencer and being a known meth user in possession of firearms.

title: Scientists find genetic divide between city moose and country moose
summary: A new study finds that Anchorage moose have greater genetic diversity than do moose on the Kenai Peninsula, and that the two populations have very different genetic structures.

title: Anchorage LGBT pride flag vandalized for third time in under a year
summary: A rainbow pride flag hanging outside the downtown Anchorage office of Identity Inc. was found half burned Tuesday morning.

title: Permanent drought? Study suggests some US states could run out of groundwater in 30 years.
summary: A new study suggest the Great Plains, California's Central Valley and areas of Texas and Kansas are drawing groundwater at an unsustainable rate.

title: West softball shuts out Eagle River
summary: Rebecca Syrup had a three-inning no-hitter to lead the West softball team past Eagle River on Wednesday.

title: Anchorage embarks on big upgrade for emergency dispatch systems
summary: The Anchorage Police Department's 20-year-old dispatch and record-keeping computer systems are "being held together with chewing gum and baling wire," said APD chief Mark Mew, but big improvements are in the works.
